title: Governance-conformance checklist in the validator
context: The governance templates (consent, DSA, charter) define a set of required fields and sections (revocation path, data-use specification, lawful-basis placeholder, provenance). The validator (validate-001) should enforce these requirements so adopters get machine-readable feedback when their consent artifact is incomplete. Making non-consensual or under-governed use fail by default is a structural anti-misuse control. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-governance-001 and rare-cancer-registry-templates-validate-001.
objective: Extend the `validate` CLI with a governance-conformance checklist command that inspects an adopter's consent or governance artifact and fails if required fields are absent: revocation path, data-use specification (DUO codes), lawful-basis placeholder, and provenance. The checker should also verify that the 'not medical advice' / 'not legal advice' notices are present on patient-facing and regulatory surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: The validator's governance checklist fails an adopter's consent artifact that lacks a revocation path.
The validator's governance checklist fails an artifact that lacks a data-use specification (DUO codes).
The validator's governance checklist fails an artifact that lacks a lawful-basis placeholder.
The validator's governance checklist fails an artifact that lacks provenance on governance clauses.
The checker produces actionable failure messages identifying the specific missing field.
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green; maintainer sign-off recorded.
output: An extension to the `tools/validate` CLI adding a `governance-check` command with the conformance checklist, committed via PR with green CI.
